spec: Pixley Markdown v4 — Native Rendering & Missing Controls
thesis: Expose native Swift/SwiftUI/AppKit controls through markdown syntax. Just pipes. AI and user decide what to do with them.
design_principles: Classic markdown look — inline controls render as standard macOS controls, not glass
Glass for containers — tables and popovers get Liquid Glass treatment
Just pipes — expose the control, let AI compose

phases.name: Native Rendering Blocks
phases.description: SwiftUI blocks embedded inline in NSTextView for markdown constructs that look bad as styled text

stories.title: Table Rendering
stories.description: Detect pipe-delimited tables with separator rows, render as SwiftUI grid in Liquid Glass container inline in text flow. Interactive elements inside cells are detected and functional.
stories.write_back: Only for interactive elements within cells

stories.title: Image Rendering
stories.description: Detect ![alt](path) syntax, render actual image inline. Local file paths resolved relative to document.

stories.title: Code Block Rendering
stories.description: Detect fenced code blocks with optional language identifier, render in SwiftUI framed container with syntax highlighting and copy-to-clipboard button.

stories.title: Collapsible Section Rendering
stories.description: Detect collapsible comment markers (already in parser), render as native SwiftUI DisclosureGroup.

phases.name: New Interactive Controls
phases.description: New fill-in types using existing [[...]] detection pattern plus checkbox variant

stories.title: Slider Control
stories.description: Detect [[slide MIN-MAX]] or [[rate MIN-MAX]], render as inline NSSlider. Write-back replaces trigger with selected number.

stories.title: Color Picker
stories.description: Detect [[pick color]], show NSColorWell/ColorPicker in popover. Write-back replaces trigger with hex color.

stories.title: Stepper Control
stories.description: Detect [[pick number]] or [[pick number MIN-MAX]], render as inline NSStepper. Write-back replaces trigger with selected number.

stories.title: Toggle Switch
stories.description: Detect [[toggle]], render as inline NSSwitch. Write-back replaces trigger with on or off.

stories.title: Auditable Checkbox
stories.description: Checkbox that auto-appends timestamp when checked. Optionally prompts for notes. Replaces old Review pattern. AI writes any labels it wants — Pixley does not interpret them.
stories.trigger: Documented pattern (AI writes checkbox with audit syntax)

stories.title: File/Folder Picker Re-pick
stories.description: After [[choose file]] or [[choose folder]] is filled, the path renders as a clickable badge. Clicking re-opens NSOpenPanel. New selection replaces old path.

dropped.reason: Was a slider with high/medium/low opinions baked in
Was an auditable checkbox with keyword opinions
dropped.replacement: Auditable checkbox — AI writes any label it wants

technical_considerations: NSTextAttachmentViewProvider (macOS 12+) for embedding SwiftUI in NSTextView
Inline controls (slider, stepper, toggle) in attributed text flow
SwiftUI table handles own hit testing for interactive elements in cells
Local file paths only for images in v1 — no remote URLs
